description.p: SYNS Man is a modern, cross-platform SSH and SFTP manager built with Electron. It lets you manage multiple remote servers from a clean graphical interface — no terminal required for everyday tasks.

ul.li: SSH terminal with full xterm.js support
SFTP file manager: upload, download, delete, rename, chmod
In-app file editor with syntax highlighting (CodeMirror)
Password and SSH key authentication (id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa, *.pem)
Encrypted credential storage
Multi-color themes: Dark, Light, Ocean, Forest, Violet, Rose
In-app changelog fetched live from GitHub
Update notifications via GitHub Releases API
Login page redesigned: greeting card with avatar when username is remembered.
Password-only input when a username is already saved.
Removed Arabic UI strings; all labels are now in English.
Removed misleading switch-user button (single admin account app).
SSH key Browse button opens ~/.ssh by default and shows all files including extensionless keys.
Selecting a .pub or .ppk file now shows a warning toast.
Update notification banner re-appears correctly after login.
Removed deprecated electron-builder fields that caused schema validation errors.
Removed macOS support. Build targets are now Linux and Windows only.
Multi-color themes: Ocean, Forest, Violet, Rose.
Remember username across sessions.
Update system overhauled — GitHub API only, no background downloads.
Initial public stable release.
Theme toggle (Dark and Light) with persistent preference.
About page with Overview and Changelog tabs.
SFTP file manager with upload, download, delete, rename, chmod.
In-app file editor with CodeMirror and syntax highlighting.
Initial release with SSH terminal, server manager, and encrypted credentials.
